sense.definition: a handheld tool used to determine the presence or absence of electrical voltage in a circuit or electrical outlet

examples.x: Before starting any electrical repairs, it is important to use a voltage tester to ensure the circuit is not live.
He used the voltage tester to check if the outlet was providing power before plugging in the appliance.
The technician grabbed his voltage tester to make sure the power was turned off before replacing the fuse.
Always double-check with a voltage tester to avoid the risk of electric shock when working with exposed wiring.
The voltage tester showed no voltage in the light fixture, confirming that the power was safely off.

sense.definition: an electrical testing device that combines multiple measurement functions in one tool, typically used to measure voltage, current, and resistance in electrical circuits

examples.x: Before replacing any components, he used a multimeter to check the circuit for any electrical issues.
I always carry a multimeter when working on electrical projects to ensure everything is functioning properly.
The technician used the multimeter to measure the voltage in the power supply.
He turned off the power and connected the multimeter to the wires to measure the current.
She used the multimeter to check for a short circuit in the wiring before proceeding with the installation.

sense.definition: a device used to locate and identify specific circuit breakers in an electrical panel or distribution board

examples.x: I used the circuit breaker finder to avoid flipping all the switches when I needed to turn off power to the garage.
Before doing any electrical work, it is safer to use a circuit breaker finder to ensure the power is off.
The electrician used the circuit breaker finder to pinpoint which breaker was connected to the faulty outlet.
When I needed to reset the power to the furnace, the circuit breaker finder helped me locate the correct breaker in seconds.

sense.definition: a specialized electrical testing instrument used to measure the resistance or insulation quality of electrical insulation materials, such as wires, cables, motors, and transformers

examples.x: The electrician used a megohmmeter to check the insulation resistance of the cables before installation.
To avoid electrical hazards, a megohmmeter should be used regularly to inspect the insulation in old wiring.
When troubleshooting electrical problems, a megohmmeter can help identify issues with the insulation of wiring.
The technician used the megohmmeter to verify that the insulation on the new electrical panel was within safe limits.

sense.definition: a device used to check the electrical condition of outlets, circuits, and wiring in buildings

examples.x: Before installing new equipment, the engineer used a circuit analyzer to make sure the electrical circuits were safe.
The electrician used a circuit analyzer to check if the wiring in the new house was correctly installed.
After the power went out, the technician grabbed the circuit analyzer to diagnose the problem.
He connected the circuit analyzer to the breaker panel to see if any circuits were overloaded.

sense.definition: a flexible measuring tool consisting of a long strip of metal, cloth, or plastic with measurement markings, used to measure lengths and distances accurately

examples.x: The carpenter used a tape measure to determine the length of the wooden board needed for the shelf.
She checked the dimensions of the room with a tape measure before buying new furniture.
The tailor used a tape measure to take precise measurements for the custom-made suit.
He marked the positions on the wall with a pencil based on the measurements from the tape measure.
The surveyor used a laser tape measure for accurate distance measurements in the field.

sense.definition: a measuring and marking tool with a straight edge and a perpendicular arm, typically made of steel, used in carpentry and construction for checking right angles and laying out accurate measurements

examples.x: The carpenter used a steel square to ensure the corners of the frame were perfectly aligned.
Before cutting the wood, he marked a straight line using the steel square for accuracy.
The builder double-checked the roof angles with a steel square to avoid mistakes.
She used a steel square to measure the dimensions of the plywood sheet precisely.
The apprentice learned how to mark right angles on lumber using the steel square.

sense.definition: a handheld device that measures the moisture content in wood for woodworking and construction purposes

examples.x: The carpenter used a wood moisture meter to check if the planks were ready for sanding.
Before installing the hardwood floor, the contractor measured the moisture levels with a wood moisture meter.
The inspector used a wood moisture meter to ensure the lumber met building standards.
High readings on the wood moisture meter indicated the boards needed more time to dry.
The flooring installer recommended using a wood moisture meter to avoid cracks in the wood.

sense.definition: a carpentry tool consisting of a reel or container filled with powdered chalk and a string, used to create a straight reference line on a surface by snapping the string coated with chalk

examples.x: The carpenter used a chalk line to mark the spot where the wall studs would be installed.
Before cutting the plywood, he snapped a chalk line to ensure the edges would be perfectly straight.
The mason stretched a chalk line across the foundation to align the first row of bricks.
She used a chalk line to create a guide for painting the stripes on the wall.
The flooring installer snapped a chalk line to mark the starting point for laying the tiles.

sense.definition: a device used to measure and monitor the amount of electrical energy consumed in a building or specific electrical circuit

examples.x: The electrician used an electrical meter to check the voltage in the circuit before starting the repairs.
When troubleshooting a faulty outlet, a technician might use an electrical meter to measure resistance.
The homeowner monitored energy usage with an electrical meter to identify which appliances consumed the most power.
Before installing the new equipment, the contractor tested the system using an electrical meter.
For safety, always use an electrical meter to verify that the power is off before working on a circuit.

sense.definition: a long, slender measuring tool typically made of wood or metal and marked with measurements in yards, feet, and inches
a handheld device that utilizes electronic or magnetic sensors to detect the presence and location of wooden or metal studs hidden behind drywall or other wall materials

examples.x: He used a stud finder to make sure he drilled into the wall's studs when hanging the picture frame.
Before mounting the TV, she grabbed the stud finder to locate the studs behind the drywall.
I always use a stud finder to avoid drilling into electrical wires or pipes in the wall.
The stud finder beeped, signaling that it had found a stud in the wall.
To hang the heavy mirror properly, the handyman used a stud finder to pinpoint the studs.

sense.definition: a tool used to determine the horizontal or vertical alignment of a surface
a woodworking tool consisting of a handle and an adjustable blade that can be set and locked at any angle

examples.x: The carpenter used a sliding bevel to measure the angle of the roof before cutting the beams.
To ensure the correct angle, he adjusted the sliding bevel and locked it in place.
She used a sliding bevel to match the angles on the new door frame.
The sliding bevel helped transfer the angle from the blueprint to the wood accurately.

sense.definition: a tool that emits a visible laser beam to project a straight, level, or plumb line onto a surface, facilitating precise alignment and positioning during construction, carpentry, or other projects

examples.x: The contractor used a laser level to make sure the foundation was perfectly level before starting the construction.
I need to grab the laser level to ensure the shelves are aligned properly on the wall.
The laser level made the process of hanging pictures much easier and faster.
He set up the laser level to check if the tiles were perfectly straight before grouting.
We used the laser level to ensure the fence posts were placed at the correct height.

sense.definition: a tool consisting of a vial filled with liquid and an air bubble used to determine horizontal or vertical alignment

examples.x: The builder used a spirit level to ensure the shelf was straight on the wall.
Before hanging the picture, he checked the frame with a spirit level to make sure it was level.
The carpenter used a spirit level to verify the floorboards were even.
She placed the spirit level on the windowsill to make sure it was perfectly horizontal.
After placing the tiles, he used a spirit level to ensure they were aligned correctly.

sense.definition: a compact level with multiple vials, including horizontal, vertical, and 45-degree angles, commonly used in tight spaces for accurate leveling

examples.x: The plumber used a torpedo level to make sure the pipe was perfectly horizontal before securing it.
He grabbed the torpedo level from his toolbox to check the alignment of the shelf brackets.
She used the 45-degree vial on the torpedo level to check the angle of the stairs.
The electrician relied on the torpedo level to ensure the conduit was straight.

sense.definition: a long level with multiple vials used for measuring horizontal, vertical, and 45-degree angles, providing accurate leveling over larger surfaces

examples.x: The carpenter used the box level to make sure the wall was perfectly straight.
He placed the box level on the table to verify that it was level before setting up the equipment.
The builder checked the foundation with a box level before starting the construction.
The mason used a box level to ensure the bricks were stacked evenly.

sense.definition: a small level designed to be attached to a string or line to ensure proper alignment in a horizontal plane

examples.x: The contractor used a line level to ensure the fence posts were perfectly aligned.
I stretched the wire across the garden and hung the line level to check the slope.
The surveyor relied on the line level to make sure the new building foundation was level across the entire site.
When installing the fence, I used a line level to keep the top rails straight and level.
The landscaper used a line level to set the correct angle for the flower beds.

sense.definition: an electronic tool that utilizes sensors to measure angles and provides digital readouts of level or plumb measurements, offering precise and convenient leveling capabilities

examples.x: I used the digital level to ensure the picture frame was hung straight on the wall.
The contractor checked the foundation with a digital level to make sure it was completely level.
Using a digital level, the tiler ensured the floor tiles were laid flat and even.
With a digital level, it was simple to measure the slope of the driveway for proper drainage.

sense.definition: a surveying instrument that combines a telescope with a spirit level, used for measuring horizontal and vertical angles, as well as distances, in surveying and construction projects

examples.x: The surveyor used a transit level to measure the elevation of the land before starting the construction project.
To ensure the foundation was perfectly level, the builder relied on a transit level for accurate measurements.
Using a transit level, the construction team set the correct grade for the drainage system.
The architect used a transit level to ensure the alignment of the columns in the large building project.

sense.definition: a specialized level or angle-measuring tool used for determining the angle of slope or inclination in various applications such as forestry, construction, and surveying

examples.x: The engineer relied on a clinometer to check the angle of the roof for proper drainage.
When hiking, she used a clinometer to determine the slope of the mountain trail.
The forestry technician used a clinometer to measure the height of the tree by calculating its angle to the top.
To assess the angle of the land, the geologist used a clinometer during the site visit.

sense.definition: a precision tool used in machining and metalworking to ensure the flatness and alignment of machine surfaces, providing accurate measurements for precise machining operations

examples.x: He carefully adjusted the machine with a machinist level to guarantee precise cuts.
The technician used the machinist level to confirm the accuracy of the metal plate alignment.
Before beginning assembly, the engineer used the machinist level to check the machine’s foundation.
After installing the new equipment, they used a machinist level to make sure everything was perfectly aligned.

sense.definition: a specialized level used in masonry work, typically featuring one or two vials, and is designed to assist in aligning bricks, blocks, or other construction materials accurately
a weight attached to a string or line used by plumbers and builders to establish vertical alignment and ensure accuracy in construction

examples.x: ChatGPT The construction worker used a plumb bob to ensure the wall was perfectly straight.
The mason relied on a plumb bob to align the bricks accurately during the build.
The carpenter attached a plumb bob to the ceiling to measure the height of the walls.
He adjusted the beam while watching the plumb bob to ensure it was level.

sense.definition: a heavy-duty level commonly used in carpentry and woodworking, featuring multiple vials for precise leveling of surfaces, ensuring accuracy in construction and installation projects
a test measuring the consistency of a concrete mix, mainly performed in a lab or at the site of construction

examples.x: The contractor performed a concrete slump test to ensure the mix was the right consistency for the foundation.
After the concrete slump test, the crew adjusted the water content to achieve the desired workability.
They used the concrete slump test to verify that the concrete would flow easily into the mold.

sense.definition: an electronic surveying instrument that combines an electronic theodolite for measuring angles and a distance meter for measuring distances

examples.x: The surveyor used a total station to accurately measure the distance between two points on the construction site.
The team set up the total station to create an accurate map of the land before starting the project.
The construction crew relied on the total station to ensure all corners of the building were perfectly square.
The total station provided real-time data to the surveyor, making it easier to adjust measurements as the project progressed.

sense.definition: precision optical instrument used for measuring horizontal and vertical angles in surveying and engineering applications

examples.x: The surveyor used a theodolite to measure the angles between the reference points on the construction site.
The construction crew relied on the theodolite to ensure the foundation was perfectly level before laying the first brick.
The team used a theodolite to accurately map the terrain before starting the road construction project.
By using a theodolite, the surveyor was able to determine the exact position of the landmark on the map.

sense.definition: a device used to measure the consistency or firmness of materials by assessing the depth of penetration of a probe or needle into the substance

examples.x: Engineers often use a penetrometer to test the strength of concrete at construction sites.
The soil scientist used a penetrometer to measure the compaction of the ground before planting crops.
Before laying the foundation, the construction team used a penetrometer to ensure the ground was stable.
The results from the penetrometer showed that the soil had good drainage properties.
The penetrometer indicated that the soil was too compacted, which could affect plant growth.

sense.definition: a device used to assess the compaction properties and density of soil or asphalt by delivering a standardized impact to the surface and measuring the resulting resistance or penetration

examples.x: The contractor used a compaction hammer to ensure the soil was tightly packed before laying the foundation.
Before laying the pavement, workers used a compaction hammer to reduce the air gaps in the soil.
The operator carefully used the compaction hammer to level the surface and prevent future settling.
The team noticed the ground became firmer after several passes with the compaction hammer.

sense.definition: a graduated rod used in surveying to determine differences in elevation or establish benchmarks

examples.x: The surveyor used the leveling rod to measure the height difference between the two points on the construction site.
The engineer carefully read the measurements on the leveling rod to verify the height of the drainage system.
After setting up the total station, the crew member held the leveling rod in place while the surveyor took the readings.
By using the leveling rod and the transit, the team was able to accurately measure the slope of the land.

sense.definition: a surveying instrument used to establish horizontal levels and measure height differences

examples.x: The surveyor used a dumpy level to check the level of the ground before starting the foundation work.
The dumpy level helped the engineers establish a consistent grade for the road construction project.
The construction crew checked the level of the floor with a dumpy level before laying down tiles.
A dumpy level is essential for precise leveling when preparing a site for a new building.

sense.definition: a wheel-like device with a calibrated measuring mechanism used to measure distances on the ground

examples.x: She walked along the path with a measuring wheel to determine how much material would be needed for the walkway.
He pulled the measuring wheel across the field to find the exact distance from one end to the other.
The team used a measuring wheel to assess the size of the area for the new playground installation.
The landscaper relied on the measuring wheel to accurately measure the perimeter of the garden.
The contractor used a measuring wheel to calculate the length of the fence line.

sense.definition: a measuring instrument used to measure and draw angles

examples.x: The teacher used a protractor to show how to measure angles in the geometry lesson.
To build the frame correctly, the carpenter needed a protractor to check the angle between the two beams.
The protractor helped the student draw precise angles for the math assignment.
Before cutting the wood, the contractor used a protractor to ensure the angle was exactly 45 degrees.
The engineer used a protractor to measure the angle of the roof in the blueprint.

sense.definition: a telescopic pole with a prism on top used in surveying for accurate measurements and positioning

examples.x: The surveyor set up the prism pole at the corner of the property to measure the distance accurately.
He adjusted the length of the prism pole to ensure the prism was at the correct height for the measurement.
A prism pole is essential for collecting data when using a total station during land surveying.
The worker held the prism pole steady while the instrument operator took the measurement.
The prism pole's lightweight design made it easy to carry and adjust during the survey.

sense.definition: specialized ruler with different scales for accurately measuring and drawing scaled plans and elevations
flat surface used by architects and drafters for manual drawing and drafting

examples.x: The architect set up the blueprint on the drafting board to begin work on the new building design.
She carefully positioned the paper on the drafting board, adjusting the angle for the best view.
After gathering all his tools, the designer placed them neatly around the drafting board.
He used the drafting board to draw detailed plans for the renovation project.
She spent hours at the drafting board, refining her architectural drawings for the upcoming presentation.

sense.definition: a straightedge tool with a perpendicular crosspiece used in conjunction with a drafting board for drawing straight lines and right angles

examples.x: He placed the T-square along the edge of the drafting board to ensure the lines were level.

sense.definition: a tool used for drawing circles and arcs of various sizes
a flexible curved ruler used for drawing smooth curves and irregular shapes
a specialized ruler with multiple scales for accurately representing measurements on a drawing, such as architectural or engineering scales

examples.x: The architect used a scale ruler to measure the dimensions of the building in the blueprint.
Before starting the project, the engineer checked the measurements with a scale ruler to ensure accuracy.
The designer pulled out a scale ruler to convert the measurements on the sketch to real-world sizes.
In the drafting room, the team used a scale ruler to ensure all their drawings were to scale.
The student used a scale ruler to measure the distances on the architectural plan for the assignment.

sense.definition: a triangular ruler used for drawing straight lines and angles, commonly available in 30-60-90 and 45-45-90 degree configurations

examples.x: The architect used the drafting triangle to create the precise 45-degree angle on the blueprint.
For the building plans, the draftsman carefully measured the angles using a drafting triangle.
The engineer relied on the drafting triangle to make sure all the lines were straight and angled correctly.
The designer's drafting triangle ensured that the angles on the technical drawing were exact and aligned.

sense.definition: a mechanical or lead pencil with various lead thicknesses (e.g., 0.5mm, 0.7mm) for precise and consistent lines

examples.x: He always carries a drafting pencil in his bag to make sure he's ready for any last-minute sketches.
The architect used a drafting pencil to carefully outline the building's floor plan.
She prefers a 2H drafting pencil because it creates fine, precise lines for her technical drawings.
The drafting pencil's thin lead made it easy to add intricate details to the design.
For this project, a drafting pencil with a harder lead was required to maintain accuracy over long hours of drawing.

sense.definition: a rectangular or octagonal-shaped pencil with a flat, wide lead, commonly used in woodworking and carpentry for marking and drawing lines on various materials

examples.x: The carpenter used a sharp carpenter pencil to mark the wood before cutting.
Make sure to use a carpenter pencil for accurate measurements when framing the house.
The carpenter pencil’s flat shape made it easy to hold while working on the project.
He reached for his carpenter pencil to draw a straight line on the wooden beam.
